2605.21442.title: torchtune: PyTorch native post-training library
2605.21442.paper_summary: Modern LLMs typically require multistage training pipelines to achieve strong downstream performance, with post-training serving as the main interface for adapting open-weight models. We introduce torchtune, a PyTorch-native library designed to streamline the post-training lifecycle of LLMs, enabling efficient fine-tuning, experimentation, and deployment-oriented workflows. Unlike many existing fine-tuning frameworks, which often optimize for ease of use, specialized recipes, or hardware efficiency at the cost of transparency and extensibility, torchtune emphasizes modularity, hackability, and direct access to the underlying PyTorch components. In this paper, we present the design principles behind torchtune, describe how they are reflected in its model builders, training recipes, and distributed training stack, and evaluate the library across representative post-training settings. We compare against popular fine-tuning frameworks, including Axolotl and Unsloth, and show that torchtune provides strong performance and memory efficiency across many settings while remaining flexible enough for rapid research iteration. These results position torchtune as a practical foundation for reproducible LLMs post-training research.
2605.21442.paper_summary_zh: 現代的 LLM 通常需要多階段的訓練流程來實現強大的下游性能，後期訓練作為適應開放權重模型的主要介面。我們介紹了 torchtune，這是一個基於 PyTorch 的庫，旨在簡化 LLM 的後期訓練生命周期，使高效的微調、實驗和部署導向的工作流程成為可能。與許多現有的微調框架不同，這些框架通常在易用性、專門食譜或硬體效率上進行優化，但卻犧牲了透明性和可擴展性，torchtune 強調模組化、可黑客性和對底層 PyTorch 組件的直接訪問。在本文中，我們介紹了 torchtune 的設計原則，描述它們如何體現在其模型構建器、訓練食譜和分佈式訓練堆棧中，並在代表性的後期訓練設置中評估該庫。我們與流行的微調框架進行比較，包括 Axolotl 和 Unsloth，並展示了 torchtune 在許多設置中提供強大的性能和內存效率，同時保持足夠的靈活性以支持快速的研究迭代。這些結果使 torchtune 成為可重現 LLM 後期訓練研究的實用基礎。
2605.21442.author: Mark Obozov et.al.
2605.21442.authors: Mark Obozov, Maxime Griot, Joseph Cummings, Evan Smothers, Felipe Mello, Rafi Ayub, Philip John Bontrager, Salman Mohammadi, Ariel Kwiatkowski, Nathan Azrak, Mircea Mironenco
